## Slice Plan Excerpt
Source: `.gsd/milestones/M001/slices/S09/S09-PLAN.md`
**Goal:** Deliver the Downgrade Manager workflow as a faithful Slint modal opened from Overview or Tools, with reference file status, backup and delta cleanup preferences, inline plan confirmation, safe backup/download/xdelta execution, visible log/progress feedback, and non-blocking worker handoff.
**Demo:** User can open and run Downgrade Manager from Overview or Tools with backup and delta cleanup preferences respected and visible status/errors.

### Slice Verification
- Adds structured tracing for downgrader open/status/plan/run phases, request ids, file-level plan and execution outcomes, settings-save failures, download/apply failures, stale-event rejection, and worker spawn failures. The modal log remains the primary user-visible surface with reference-style per-file messages; diagnostics stay in tests/logs rather than UI text.



## UNIT: Execute Task T03 ("Implement confirmed executor and write seams") — Slice S09 ("S09"), Milestone M001

## Context Mode

Lane: **execution lane**.
Use `gsd_exec` for builds, tests, and diagnostics, `gsd_exec_search` before reruns, and `gsd_resume` after compaction or resume.

## Context Snapshot
Source: `.gsd/last-snapshot.md`

# GSD context snapshot (2026-05-18T02:51:02.113Z)

## Top project memories
- [MEM003] (pattern) Settings UI persistence is mediated by SettingsController, which owns the last successfully persisted AppSettings snapshot. Slint callbacks optimistically change the visible property, then Rust saves and returns the value Slint must display, allowing failed writes to revert the UI to persisted state.
- [MEM009] (architecture) Platform OS access is isolated behind fakeable traits in src/platform. Real discovery/open adapters should use crate/native APIs where practical and return typed PlatformError or DesktopActionResult values; non-Windows real adapters intentionally return UnsupportedPlatform while domain models and fake-backed tests remain cross-platform.
- [MEM013] (pattern) Background work in this port should emit owned WorkerEvent envelopes through WorkerEventSink implementations. Use RecordingEventSink in tests and SlintEventLoopSink for UI handoff so worker closures never mutate Slint component handles or models directly.
- [MEM025] (pattern) S04 established the Overview data-flow pattern: pure domain `OverviewSnapshot` and Slint-free `OverviewController`, adapter-backed collection/update/link services, owned worker payloads, and UI mutation only through the Slint event-loop sink. Future tabs should preserve this separation so filesystem, network, and desktop work remain fakeable and non-blocking.
- [MEM014] (pattern) For S03 discovery work, keep pure domain contracts, platform facts, and service orchestration separate: domain modules do not touch OS/Slint, platform access goes through fakeable traits, and DiscoveryService records ordered attempts/manager steps without showing UI prompts. MO2 configuration failures are intentionally manager-specific and should not be hidden by falling through to unrelated game-path sources.
- [MEM002] (gotcha) Settings-tab UI intentionally exposes a `Warning` log-level radio even though `CMT/src/tabs/_settings.py` lists only Debug/Info/Error; the Rust port derive
…[truncated]

## Handoff from plan-slice

**Intent:** Completed plan-slice for M001/S09

---

## Resume State
- No continue file present. Start from the top of the task plan.

## Carry-Forward Context
- `.gsd/milestones/M001/slices/S09/tasks/T01-SUMMARY.md` — T01: Added a pure downgrader domain contract with reference labels, CRC maps, backup/patch helpers, and typed row payloads. | Added a pure downgrader domain contract with reference labels, CRC maps, backup/patch helpers, and typed row payloads. | decisions: Kept the Downgrade Manager source of truth in a dedicated `domain::downgrader` module so later S09 service/controller/UI work can consume exact reference strings, CRC classifications, and helper semantics without duplicating them or touching Slint/IO. | key_files: src/domain/downgrader.rs; src/domain/mod.rs
- `.gsd/milestones/M001/slices/S09/tasks/T02-SUMMARY.md` — T02: Added a read-only DowngraderService that builds CRC status snapshots and inline preview plans without mutation. | Added a read-only DowngraderService that builds CRC status snapshots and inline preview plans without mutation. | decisions: Kept Downgrader status/preview planning read-only over the existing `Filesystem` trait so mutation, download, and xdelta apply behavior must be introduced later through separate confirmed worker seams.; Represented preview actions as structured `DowngraderPlanStepKind`/`DowngraderPlanStep` domain payloads rather than UI strings alone, preserving testable plan semantics for later modal confirmation work. | key_files: src/services/downgrader.rs; src/services/mod.rs; src/domain/downgrader.rs

## Inlined Task Plan (authoritative local execution contract)
Source: `.gsd/milestones/M001/slices/S09/tasks/T03-PLAN.md`

---
estimated_steps: 15
estimated_files: 5
skills_used: []

# T03: Implement confirmed executor and write seams

---
estimated_steps: 9
estimated_files: 6
skills_used:
  - rust-async-patterns
  - tdd
  - security-review
  - verify-before-complete
---
Why: After inline confirmation, S09 must safely execute the reference backup, restore, download, xdelta apply, and cleanup semantics against sandbox fixtures before real game paths are reachable.
Do: Add a separate writable filesystem trait to `src/platform/filesystem.rs` instead of expanding the existing read-only `Filesystem` contract. Extend `PlatformOperation` with safe write/copy/rename/remove labels. Implement real filesystem mutation methods with typed `PlatformError` mapping and local fake/recording implementations for tests. In `src/services/downgrader.rs`, add `DeltaDownloader` and `DeltaApplier` traits, a reqwest-backed downloader with bounded progress callbacks, and a production xdelta applier only after adding/proving a compatible dependency with a tiny deterministic fixture. Add confirmed execution that revalidates current file and backup CRCs immediately before each mutation, processes the six files independently, creates/reuses/removes `_downgradeBackup` and `_upgradeBackup` files according to direction and `Keep Backups`, downloads only the needed `NG-to-OG-{file}.xdelta` or `OG-to-NG-{file}.xdelta`, applies deltas with the current backup as input, honors `Delete Patches`, continues after per-file failures where safe, and never deletes the only valid source backup after a failed apply.
Failure Modes Q5: File write/copy/rename/remove errors log `Failed patching {file}` and continue where safe. Download timeout/request failure and malformed or failed xdelta apply log failure and preserve backups. If no proven production xdelta applier exists, fail the task and replan rather than shipping a silently non-functional production path.
Load Profile Q6: Fixed six-file execution, bounded patch downloads, sequential mutation, and progress events per file/download prevent unbounded concurrency or memory growth.
Negative Tests Q7: Cover valid desired-backup restore with and without `Keep Backups`, invalid backup deletion, current-backup creation/reuse, as-needed delta download, delete-deltas cleanup, failed download, failed apply, read-only/permission failures, unsupported source generation, and no downloader/applier calls for skipped files.
Done when: Executor tests prove reference log rows and mutation/download/apply calls for success and failure paths, and the production applier has a fixture proof if a dependency is added.

## Inputs

- `src/services/downgrader.rs`
- `src/platform/mod.rs`
- `src/platform/filesystem.rs`
- `src/domain/downgrader.rs`
- `Cargo.toml`
- `Cargo.lock`
- `CMT/src/downgrader.py`

## Expected Output

- `src/platform/mod.rs`
- `src/platform/filesystem.rs`
- `src/services/downgrader.rs`

- `Cargo.lock`

## Verification

cargo test downgrader_executor

## Observability Impact

Adds execution tracing and file-level result diagnostics for backup, restore, download, apply, cleanup, permission, and xdelta failures while keeping the modal log user-safe.

## Execution Rules

1. Tersely narrate transitions, decisions, and verification outcomes between tool-call clusters.
2. Use the injected memory/context blocks first. Call `memory_query` with 2-4 keywords from the task title and touched files only when no injected memory block exists or the inlined memory/context is insufficient for this task.

4. Execute the task plan. Before any `Write` that creates an artifact or output file, check whether that path already exists. If it does, read it first and decide whether the work is already done, should be extended, or truly needs replacement.
5. Build real behavior through the intended surface; stubs/mocks belong in tests only.
6. Add or update tests. Tests must use git-tracked files or inline fixtures, never .gitignore/gitignored local paths such as `.gsd/`, `.planning/`, or `.audits/`.
7. Preserve useful observability for non-trivial async, API, background, or error-path work.

**Background process rule:** never run bare `command &`. Redirect output first, e.g. `command > /dev/null 2>&1 &`, or use `bg_shell` when available.

## Gates And Verification

- If task sections exist for Failure Modes (Q5), Load Profile (Q6), Negative Tests (Q7), or Observability Impact, implement and verify them.
- Verify must-haves with concrete commands or observable behavior.
- Run slice-level verification from the slice plan. Final tasks need all checks passing; intermediate tasks should record partial passes.
- Populate `## Verification Evidence` with `formatEvidenceTable` rows: command, exit code, verdict, duration. If no checks were found, say so.
- For UI/browser/DOM/user-visible web changes, exercise the real flow and record explicit checks.

If verification fails, use one-hypothesis-at-a-time debugging: state the hypothesis, test it, change one variable, read complete functions/imports, separate facts from assumptions, and stop after 3 failed fixes to reset the model.

Keep about **~160K chars** for verification and summary. If context is nearly spent, stop implementation and write a resumable summary.

## Completion Contract

- If the plan is fundamentally invalid, set `blocker_discovered: true` in the summary and explain.
- For downstream-impacting ambiguity that cannot be resolved from code, plans, or decisions, include an `escalation` object with question, options, recommendation, rationale, and `continueWithDefault`.
- Capture meaningful architecture/pattern/observability decisions with `capture_thought`; capture non-obvious gotchas or conventions only when they save future investigation.
- Use the inlined Task Summary template below. Read `C:\Users\evild\.gsd\agent\extensions\gsd\templates\task-summary.md` only if the inlined template is absent or visibly truncated.
- Call `gsd_task_complete` with camelCase fields `milestoneId`, `sliceId`, `taskId`, `oneLiner`, `narrative`, `verification`, and `verificationEvidence`.
- The DB-backed tool is the canonical write path. Do **not** manually write `J:\cmt-rs\.gsd\milestones\M001\slices\S09\tasks\T03-SUMMARY.md` or edit PLAN.md checkboxes; the tool renders the summary and updates state.
- Do not run git commands; the system commits from your summary.

[package]
build = "build.rs"
edition = "2024"
name = "cmt-rs"
rust-version = "1.85.0"
version = "0.1.0"

[dependencies]
anyhow = "1.0.102"
arboard = "3.6.1"
crc32fast = "1.5.0"
directories = "6.0.0"
pelite = "0.10.0"
reqwest = {version = "0.13.3", default-features = false, features = ["json", "rustls"]}
serde = {version = "1.0.228", features = ["derive"]}
serde_json = "1.0.149"
slint = "1.16.1"
sysinfo = "0.39.2"
thiserror = "2.0.18"
tokio = {version = "1.52.3", features = ["rt-multi-thread", "macros", "sync"]}
toml = "1.1.2"
tracing = "0.1.44"
tracing-subscriber = {version = "0.3.23", features = ["env-filter"]}
walkdir = "2.5.0"

[target.'cfg(windows)'.dependencies]
windows = {version = "0.62.2", features = ["Win32_Storage_FileSystem", "Win32_UI_Shell", "Win32_UI_WindowsAndMessaging"]}
windows-registry = "0.6.1"

#

import queue
import stat
from pathlib import Path
from shutil import copy2
from threading import Thread
from tkinter import *
from tkinter import ttk
from types import MappingProxyType
from typing import TYPE_CHECKING

import pyxdelta
import requests
from tktooltip import ToolTip  # type: ignore[reportMissingTypeStubs]

from enums import LogType, Tab
from globals import *
from logger import Logger
from modal_window import AboutWindow, ModalWindow
from utils import (
	get_crc32,
	is_file,
)

if TYPE_CHECKING:
	from helpers import (
		CMCheckerInterface,
	)

COLOR_OG = "dodger blue"
COLOR_NG = "SlateBlue1"
COLOR_AE = "salmon"
PATCH_URL_BASE = "https://github.com/wxMichael/Collective-Modding-Toolkit/releases/download/delta-patches/"


class Downgrader(ModalWindow):
	CRCs_game = MappingProxyType({
		"Fallout4.exe": {
			"97DA3E03": InstallType.Obsolete,  # 1.10.120
			"2ED2A242": InstallType.Obsolete,  # 1.10.130
			"A0100017": InstallType.Obsolete,  # 1.10.138
			"9ABC94F0": InstallType.Obsolete,  # 1.10.162
			"C6053902": InstallType.OG,  # 1.10.163
			"B61675B1": InstallType.Obsolete,  # 1.10.980
			"C5965A2E": InstallType.NG,  # 1.10.984
			"0AEB19A7": InstallType.Obsolete,  # 1.11.137
			"1E90BE57": InstallType.Obsolete,  # 1.11.159
			"0481725D": InstallType.Obsolete,  # 1.11.169a
			"0E176ABC": InstallType.Obsolete,  # 1.11.169b
			"CF47788D": InstallType.AE,  # 1.11.191
		},
		"Fallout4Launcher.exe": {
			"02445570": InstallType.OG,  # 1.10.120 to 1.10.163
			"F6A06FF5": InstallType.NG,  # 1.10.980 to 1.10.984
			"0E696744": InstallType.Obsolete,  # 1.11.137
			"D15C6A49": InstallType.Obsolete,  # 1.11.159
			"8C52BE93": InstallType.Obsolete,  # 1.11.169a
			"591009C9": InstallType.Obsolete,  # 1.11.169b
			"720BB9C3": InstallType.AE,  # 1.11.191
		},
		"steam_api64.dll": {
			"BBD912FC": InstallType.OG,
			"E36E7B4D": InstallType.NGAE,
		},
	})
	CRCs_ck = MappingProxyType({
		"CreationKit.exe": {
			"0F5C065B": InstallType.OG,
			"481CCE95": InstallType.NG,
			"49E45284": InstallType.AE,
		},
		"Tools\\Archive2\\Archive2.exe": {
			"4CDFC7B5": InstallType.OG,
			"71A5240B": InstallType.NG,
			"C867674F": InstallType.AE,
		},
		"Tools\\Archive2\\Archive2Interop.dll": {
			"850D36A9": InstallType.OG,
			"EFBE3622": InstallType.NG,
			"7B893B0D": InstallType.AE,
		},
	})
	CRCs_by_type: MappingProxyType[InstallType, list[str]] = MappingProxyType({
		InstallType.Obsolete: [],
		InstallType.OG: [],
		InstallType.NG: [],
		InstallType.AE: [],
	})
	for CRCs in list(CRCs_game.values()) + list(CRCs_ck.values()):
		for crc, install_type in CRCs.items():
			if install_type == InstallType.NGAE:
				CRCs_by_type[InstallType.NG].append(crc)
				CRCs_by_type[InstallType.AE].append(crc)
			else:
				CRCs_by_type[install_type].append(crc)

	def __init__(self, parent: Wm, cmc: CMCheckerInterface) -> None:
		super().__init__(parent, cmc, "Downgrader", 600, 334)

		self.current_versions: dict[str, InstallType] = {}
		self.unknown_game = False
		self.unknown_ck = False

		self.version_labels: list[ttk.Label] = []

		self.download_queue: queue.Queue[tuple[str, Path, Path]] = queue.Queue()
		self.download_progress_updates: queue.Queue[float] = queue.Queue()
		self.download_or_patch_in_progress = False
		self.download_thread: Thread | None = None

		self.get_info()

		self.bv_wants_downgrade = BooleanVar(value=self.current_versions["Fallout4.exe"] == InstallType.OG)
		self.bv_keep_backups = BooleanVar(value=cmc.settings.dict["downgrader_keep_backups"])
		self.bv_delete_deltas = BooleanVar(value=cmc.settings.dict["downgrader_delete_deltas"])

		self.build_gui()

	def get_info(self) -> None:
		self.unknown_game = False
		self.unknown_ck = False
		for file_name, file_crcs in list(Downgrader.CRCs_game.items()) + list(Downgrader.CRCs_ck.items()):
			file_path = self.cmc.game.game_path / file_name
			if is_file(file_path):
				crc = get_crc32(file_path)
				self.current_versions[file_name] = file_crcs.get(crc, InstallType.Unknown)
			else:
				self.current_versions[file_name] = InstallType.NotFound

			if self.current_versions[file_name] in {InstallType.Unknown, InstallType.NotFound}:
				if file_name in self.CRCs_game:
					self.unknown_game = True
				else:
					self.unknown_ck = True

	def build_gui(self) -> None:
		self.grid_columnconfigure(2, weight=1)
		self.frame_game = ttk.Labelframe(self, text="Current Game", padding=5)
		self.frame_game.grid(column=0, row=0, rowspan=2, sticky=NSEW, padx=10)
		self.frame_game.grid_columnconfigure(0, weight=1)
		self.frame_ck = ttk.Labelframe(self, text="Current Creation Kit", padding=5)
		self.frame_ck.grid(column=0, row=2, rowspan=2, sticky=NSEW, padx=10)
		self.frame_ck.grid_columnconfigure(0, weight=1)

		file_names_game = "\n".join([f"{Path(f).name}:" for f in self.CRCs_game])
		file_names_ck = "\n".join([f"{Path(f).name}:" for f in self.CRCs_ck])

		label_file_names_game = ttk.Label(self.frame_game, text=file_names_game, font=FONT, justify=RIGHT)
		label_file_names_game.grid(column=0, row=0, rowspan=len(self.CRCs_game), sticky=E, padx=5)

		label_file_names_ck = ttk.Label(self.frame_ck, text=file_names_ck, font=FONT, justify=RIGHT)
		label_file_names_ck.grid(column=0, row=0, rowspan=len(self.CRCs_ck), sticky=E, padx=5)

		self.draw_versions()

		frame_radio_desired = ttk.Labelframe(self, text="Desired Version", padding=5)
		frame_radio_desired.grid(column=1, row=0, rowspan=2, sticky=NSEW, padx=5)

		ttk.Radiobutton(
			frame_radio_desired,
			text="Old-Gen",
			variable=self.bv_wants_downgrade,
			value=True,
		).grid(column=0, row=0, sticky=NSEW)

		ttk.Radiobutton(
			frame_radio_desired,
			text="Next-Gen",
			variable=self.bv_wants_downgrade,
			value=False,
		).grid(column=0, row=1, sticky=NSEW)

		frame_options = ttk.Labelframe(self, text="Options", padding=5)
		frame_options.grid(column=1, row=2, rowspan=2, sticky=NSEW, padx=5)
		self.check_keep_backups = ttk.Checkbutton(
			frame_options,
			text="Keep Backups",
			variable=self.bv_keep_backups,
			padding=0,
		)
		self.check_keep_backups.grid(column=0, row=0, sticky=W, pady=5)
		ToolTip(self.check_keep_backups, TOOLTIP_DOWNGRADER_BACKUPS)

		self.check_delete_deltas = ttk.Checkbutton(
			frame_options,
			text="Delete Patches",
			variable=self.bv_delete_deltas,
			padding=0,
		)
		self.check_delete_deltas.grid(column=0, row=1, sticky=W, pady=5)
		ToolTip(self.check_delete_deltas, TOOLTIP_DOWNGRADER_DELTAS)

		self.button_patch = ttk.Button(self, text="Patch\n All", command=self.patch_files)
		self.button_patch.grid(column=2, row=0, rowspan=2, sticky=NSEW, padx=(5, 10), pady=(10, 0))

		ttk.Button(
			self,
			text="About",
			command=lambda: AboutWindow(self, self.cmc, 500, 300, ABOUT_DOWNGRADING_TITLE, ABOUT_DOWNGRADING),
		).grid(column=2, row=2, sticky=EW, padx=(5, 10), pady=(5, 0))

		frame_bottom = ttk.Frame(self)
		frame_bottom.grid(column=0, row=5, columnspan=3, sticky=EW, pady=(5, 0))
		frame_bottom.grid_columnconfigure(0, weight=1)
		self.logger = Logger(frame_bottom)

		self.logger.log_message(LogType.Info, "Patches will be downloaded and applied as-needed.", skip_logging=True)

		self.progress_var = DoubleVar()
		self.progress_bar = ttk.Progressbar(self, variable=self.progress_var, maximum=100)
		self.progress_bar.grid(column=0, row=6, columnspan=3, sticky=EW, ipady=1)

	def draw_versions(self) -> None:
		for label in self.version_labels:
			label.destroy()

		frame = self.frame_game
		i = 0
		for file_name, install_type in self.current_versions.items():
			if file_name == "CreationKit.exe":
				frame = self.frame_ck
				i = 0

			install_type_string = install_type

			if install_type == InstallType.AE:
				color = COLOR_AE
			elif install_type == InstallType.NGAE:
				if self.cmc.game.is_foae():
					color = COLOR_AE
					install_type_string = InstallType.AE
				elif self.cmc.game.is_fong():
					color = COLOR_NG
					install_type_string = InstallType.NG
				else:
					color = COLOR_BAD
			elif install_type == InstallType.NG:
				color = COLOR_NG
			elif install_type == InstallType.NotFound:
				color = COLOR_NEUTRAL_1
			elif install_type in {InstallType.Unknown, InstallType.Obsolete}:
				color = COLOR_BAD
			else:
				color = COLOR_OG

			label = ttk.Label(
				frame,
				text=install_type_string,
				font=FONT,
				foreground=color,
				justify=RIGHT,
			)
			label.grid(column=1, row=i, sticky=E, padx=5)
			self.version_labels.append(label)
			i += 1

	def patch_files(self) -> None:
		self.button_patch.configure(state=DISABLED)
		self.logger.clear()

		settings = self.cmc.settings
		resave = False
		if settings.dict["downgrader_keep_backups"] != self.bv_keep_backups.get():
			settings.dict["downgrader_keep_backups"] = self.bv_keep_backups.get()
			resave = True
		if settings.dict["downgrader_delete_deltas"] != self.bv_delete_deltas.get():
			settings.dict["downgrader_delete_deltas"] = self.bv_delete_deltas.get()
			resave = True
		if resave:
			settings.save()

		desired_version = InstallType.OG if self.bv_wants_downgrade.get() else InstallType.NG

		patch_needed = False
		for file_name, install_type in self.current_versions.items():
			patch_needed = False
			file_path = self.cmc.game.game_path / file_name

			match install_type:
				case desired_version.value:
					self.logger.log_message(LogType.Info, f"Skipped {file_path.name}: Already {desired_version}.")
					continue

				case InstallType.NotFound:
					self.logger.log_message(LogType.Info, f"Skipped {file_path.name}: Not Found.")
					continue

				case InstallType.AE:
					self.logger.log_message(LogType.Info, f"Skipped {file_path.name}: Unsupported Version.")
					continue

				case InstallType.Obsolete:
					self.logger.log_message(LogType.Info, f"Skipped {file_path.name}: Unsupported Version.")
					continue

				case _:
					patch_needed = True
					self.patch_file(file_path, desired_version)

		if not patch_needed:
			self.button_patch.configure(state=NORMAL)

	def patch_file(self, file_path: Path, desired_version: InstallType) -> None:
		backup_name_og = f"{file_path.stem}_upgradeBackup{file_path.suffix}"
		backup_name_ng = f"{file_path.stem}_downgradeBackup{file_path.suffix}"

		current_crc = get_crc32(file_path)

		if desired_version == InstallType.OG:
			if current_crc not in self.CRCs_by_type[InstallType.NG]:
				self.logger.log_message(LogType.Info, f"Skipped {file_path.name}: Unsupported Version.")
				return

			patch_direction = "NG-to-OG-"
			backup_file_name_desired = backup_name_og
			backup_file_name_current = backup_name_ng
		else:
			if current_crc not in self.CRCs_by_type[InstallType.OG]:
				self.logger.log_message(LogType.Info, f"Skipped {file_path.name}: Unsupported Version.")
				return

			patch_direction = "OG-to-NG-"
			backup_file_name_desired = backup_name_ng
			backup_file_name_current = backup_name_og

		backup_file_path_desired = file_path.with_name(backup_file_name_desired)
		backup_file_path_current = file_path.with_name(backup_file_name_current)

		try:
			if file_path.stat().st_file_attributes & stat.FILE_ATTRIBUTE_READONLY:
				file_path.chmod(stat.S_IWRITE)

			if is_file(backup_file_path_current):
				print("Backup of current version exists.")
				if get_crc32(backup_file_path_current) == get_crc32(file_path):
					print(f"Backup CRC good. Deleting {file_path.name}")
					file_path.unlink()
				else:
					print(f"Backup CRC bad. Deleting {backup_file_path_current.name}")
					backup_file_path_current.unlink()

			if is_file(file_path):
				print(f"Backing up {file_path.name} to {backup_file_path_current.name}")
				file_path.rename(backup_file_path_current)

			if is_file(backup_file_path_desired):
				print(f"{backup_file_path_desired.name} exists.")
				if get_crc32(backup_file_path_desired) in self.CRCs_by_type[desired_version]:
					print(f"Backup CRC good. Restoring to {file_path.name}")
					if self.bv_keep_backups.get():
						copy2(backup_file_path_desired, file_path)
					else:
						backup_file_path_desired.replace(file_path)
					self.logger.log_message(LogType.Good, f"Patched {file_path.name}")

				else:
					print(f"Backup CRC bad. Deleting {backup_file_path_desired.name}")
					backup_file_path_desired.unlink()

			if not is_file(file_path):
				print("Restore from backup not possible. Patch download needed.")
				url = f"{PATCH_URL_BASE}{patch_direction}{file_path.name}.xdelta"
				self.download_queue.put((url, backup_file_path_current, file_path))
			elif not self.bv_keep_backups.get():
				backup_file_path_current.unlink()

		except OSError as e:
			print("Restore failed due to exception.", e)
			self.logger.log_message(LogType.Bad, f"Failed patching {file_path.name}")

		if not self.download_or_patch_in_progress:
			self.download_or_patch_in_progress = True
			self.processing_data = True
			self.check_download_queue()

	def check_download_queue(self) -> None:
		try:
			next_download = self.download_queue.get_nowait()
		except queue.Empty:
			print("Queue empty...")
			self.cmc.refresh_tab(Tab.Overview)
			self.get_info()
			self.draw_versions()
			self.button_patch.configure(state=NORMAL)
			self.download_or_patch_in_progress = False
			self.processing_data = False
			return

		file_path = Path(Path(next_download[0]).name)
		if is_file(file_path):
			self.download_thread = None
			self.progress_var.set(100)
		else:
			self.progress_var.set(0)
			self.download_thread = Thread(target=self._threaded_download, args=(next_download[0],))
			self.download_thread.start()
		self.check_download_progress(*next_download)

	def _threaded_download(self, url: str) -> None:
		file_path = Path(Path(url).name)

		response = requests.get(url, timeout=10, stream=True)
		total_size = int(response.headers.get("content-length", 0))
		downloaded_size = 0
		with file_path.open("wb") as f:
			for data in response.iter_content(chunk_size=1024):
				downloaded_size += len(data)
				f.write(data)
				self.download_progress_updates.put(downloaded_size / total_size * 100)
		self.download_progress_updates.put(100)
		self.download_thread = None

	def check_download_progress(self, url: str, infile: Path, outfile: Path) -> None:
		while self.download_progress_updates.qsize():
			try:
				value = self.download_progress_updates.get()
			except queue.Empty:
				break
			else:
				self.progress_var.set(value)

		if self.download_thread is None:
			print("Download completed. Patching...")
			self.apply_patch(url, infile, outfile)
			return
		self.cmc.root.after(100, self.check_download_progress, url, infile, outfile)

	def apply_patch(self, url: str, infile: Path, outfile: Path) -> None:
		patch_name = Path(url).name
		print(f"Applying {patch_name} to {infile.name}")
		patch_successful = pyxdelta.decode(
			str(infile),
			patch_name,
			str(outfile),
		)

		if patch_successful:
			self.logger.log_message(LogType.Good, f"Patched {outfile.name}")
		else:
			self.logger.log_message(LogType.Bad, f"Failed patching {outfile.name}")

		if not self.bv_keep_backups.get():
			infile.unlink()

		if self.bv_delete_deltas.get():
			Path(patch_name).unlink()

		self.check_download_queue()

**Assessing compatibility issues**

I'm noticing there's a potential compatibility issue with Rust 1.90 and the project's specified rust version of 1.85. arguments.purpose: Summarize candidate xdelta crate APIs from downloaded registry source
arguments.script:
python - <<'PY'
from pathlib import Path
import os
home=Path(os.environ.get('CARGO_HOME', Path.home()/'.cargo'))
for crate in ['xdelta3-0.1.5','vcdiff-decoder-1.0.0']:
    roots=list((home/'registry'/'src').glob(f'*/{crate}'))
    print(f'## {crate}')
    if not roots:
        print('not found')
        continue
    root=roots[0]
    for rel in ['src/lib.rs','README.md']:
        path=root/rel
        if path.exists():
            text=path.read_text(errors='ignore')
            lines=[]
            for line in text.splitlines():
                if any(tok in line for tok in ['pub ', 'fn ', 'struct ', 'enum ', 'trait ', 'decode', 'Decoder', 'encode']):
                    lines.append(line[:220])
            print(f'-- {rel} --')
            print('\n'.join(lines[:120]))
PY

# vcdiff-decoder
vcdiff-decoder is a library that provides a VCDIFF decoder implementation. VCDIFF (Delta Compression Format) is a format for encoding and decoding differences between files.
This library allows you to decode VCDIFF files and apply the differences to generate the original file.

content.thinking:
**Evaluating xdelta3 and bindgen issues**

I'm looking into potential issues with xdelta3 using an older version of bindgen which might not work with Rust 2024. I need to test cargo add and compile. For Windows, it might require clang, which could be a problem. Maybe using WSL is better. I’m considering modifying Cargo.toml to add xdelta3 as a dependency, and I wonder if manual edits or using gsd_exec for cargo add are the best options without causing issues with file changes.

**Assessing cargo add and filesystem traits**

I need to consider how to use `cargo add` in the current working directory without needing to change directories first. I’m planning to add xdelta3 and potentially a minimal fixture test later. If that fails, maybe I’ll remove it and use a blocker, but the instructions suggest failing or replanning if there's no production applier available. I'll also need to implement a writable filesystem trait in platform/filesystem.rs without altering the existing read-only contract. Should I create a separate trait for writable methods instead?

**Defining WritableFilesystem traits**

I'm considering how to define a `pub trait WritableFilesystem` without extending the existing read-only contract. The `DowngraderExecutor` could be generic over `F: Filesystem + WritableFilesystem`, which would help maintain read-only capabilities. I also need to include appropriate labels in `PlatformOperation` for actions like WriteFile, CopyFile, RenameFile, and RemoveFile. I'm wondering whether to add CreateDirectory too. Also, I have to think about backups in the same directory and ensure downloads write the xdelta file in the current working directory.

**Considering plan options**

I'm thinking about whether to accept `DowngraderPlanRequest` or `DowngraderPreviewPlan`. The phrase "after inline confirmation" probably means the user interface shows a preview plan, and then the executor checks the current file and backup CRCs right away. If I use the preview plan, it might be stale, but I still need to re-read it. However, I need to ensure there's a valid game root and appropriate options.

**Evaluating backup and file processes**

I’m considering a backup strategy. If a current backup exists and its CRC matches the current file’s, I should delete the current file and keep the backup. If it doesn't match, I’ll delete the backup instead. If a desired backup exists, I can either copy or replace it based on preferences. If the file is missing, I’ll queue a download from the current backup. Additionally, I need to catch any OSError logs during these processes.

**Considering backup restoration**

I’m thinking about restoring the current backup to a file if an apply fails. While it’s not essential to delete valid source backups, it could enhance UX. However, that adds extra mutation risk. I should ensure we check if the current backup is valid before proceeding. If the current file exists and deleting it fails, we wouldn’t proceed. Plus, I need to verify the CRCs of both the current file and backup before any mutations.

**Evaluating download logic**

I need to check if the patch path exists and is readable. If it does, there's no need to download it. But if it's missing, I should then proceed with the download. The DeltaDownloader trait will be useful here; maybe I need to implement a function that handles the download process with progress. It seems I could set up a downloader that writes directly to the destination while ensuring progress updates.

**Considering delta downloader implementation**

I'm exploring a trait for downloading deltas with progress callbacks. I'm thinking about a bounded patch download setup that limits the maximum bytes, like setting `MAX_DELTA_PATCH_BYTES` to 128MB. I wonder if I should use the blocking feature in `reqwest`. Current `reqwest` lacks this, and I'm pondering if implementing async might work instead.

Alternatively, if I go the trait route, I could add a "blocking" feature to `reqwest`, but I need to check how version 0.13 handles these features.

**Exploring reqwest 0.13 features**

I'm delving into the features of reqwest 0.13, especially its likely support for blocking. I wonder if I need the "rustls" feature specified as `rustls-tls`. I'll have to verify that later, but for now, I should gather documentation for `ReqwestDeltaDownloader`, especially regarding timeouts.

The `download_delta` method could use a timeout of 10 seconds and stream chunks, with progress callbacks that only trigger when percentages change by at least 1%. I’m thinking of using `std::time::Duration` and importing `reqwest::blocking::Client` for blocking operations. For files, I'm considering if the `download_delta` method could return a Vec.

**Analyzing download failure**

I'm noting a failed download situation with logs indicating a downloader failure. I need to look into why it didn't apply correctly. However, it seems the source backup is preserved, which is a silver lining. It’s possible that the patch isn't written, so I’ll need to explore further on that aspect. This will help me understand the problem better and find a solution.

**Considering Cargo updates**

I'm thinking it might be easier to edit Cargo before running `cargo test`, since that updates the lock file. The Cargo.lock should update automatically during the build unless it's frozen. I realize that the expected output needs to include Cargo.lock as part of the process. To manage dependencies effectively, I can use `cargo add` to include new ones. It's all about making sure everything is properly configured!

I’m considering modifying Cargo.toml to add xdelta3 as a dependency, and I wonder if manual edits or using gsd_exec for cargo add are the best options without causing issues with file changes."},{"type":"summary_text","text":"**Assessing cargo add and filesystem traits**\n\nI need to consider how to use `cargo add` in the current working directory without needing to change directories first. I’m planning to add xdelta3 and potentially a minimal fixture test later. If that fails, maybe I’ll remove it and use a blocker, but the instructions suggest failing or replanning if there's no production applier available. I'll also need to implement a writable filesystem trait in platform/filesystem.rs without altering the existing read-only contract. Should I create a separate trait for writable methods instead?"},{"type":"summary_text","text":"**Defining WritableFilesystem traits**\n\nI'm considering how to define a `pub trait WritableFilesystem` without extending the existing read-only contract. The `DowngraderExecutor` could be generic over `F: Filesystem + WritableFilesystem`, which would help maintain read-only capabilities. I also need to include appropriate labels in `PlatformOperation` for actions like WriteFile, CopyFile, RenameFile, and RemoveFile. I'm wondering whether to add CreateDirectory too. Also, I have to think about backups in the same directory and ensure downloads write the xdelta file in the current working directory."},{"type":"summary_text","text":"**Considering plan options**\n\nI'm thinking about whether to accept `DowngraderPlanRequest` or `DowngraderPreviewPlan`. The phrase \"after inline confirmation\" probably means the user interface shows a preview plan, and then the executor checks the current file and backup CRCs right away. If I use the preview plan, it might be stale, but I still need to re-read it. However, I need to ensure there's a valid game root and appropriate options."},{"type":"summary_text","text":"**Evaluating backup and file processes**\n\nI’m considering a backup strategy. If a current backup exists and its CRC matches the current file’s, I should delete the current file and keep the backup. If it doesn't match, I’ll delete the backup instead. If a desired backup exists, I can either copy or replace it based on preferences. If the file is missing, I’ll queue a download from the current backup. Additionally, I need to catch any OSError logs during these processes."},{"type":"summary_text","text":"**Considering backup restoration**\n\nI’m thinking about restoring the current backup to a file if an apply fails. While it’s not essential to delete valid source backups, it could enhance UX. However, that adds extra mutation risk. I should ensure we check if the current backup is valid before proceeding. If the current file exists and deleting it fails, we wouldn’t proceed. Plus, I need to verify the CRCs of both the current file and backup before any mutations."},{"type":"summary_text","text":"**Evaluating download logic**\n\nI need to check if the patch path exists and is readable. If it does, there's no need to download it. But if it's missing, I should then proceed with the download. The DeltaDownloader trait will be useful here; maybe I need to implement a function that handles the download process with progress. It seems I could set up a downloader that writes directly to the destination while ensuring progress updates."},{"type":"summary_text","text":"**Considering delta downloader implementation**\n\nI'm exploring a trait for downloading deltas with progress callbacks. I'm thinking about a bounded patch download setup that limits the maximum bytes, like setting `MAX_DELTA_PATCH_BYTES` to 128MB. I wonder if I should use the blocking feature in `reqwest`. Current `reqwest` lacks this, and I'm pondering if implementing async might work instead.\n\nAlternatively, if I go the trait route, I could add a \"blocking\" feature to `reqwest`, but I need to check how version 0.13 handles these features."},{"type":"summary_text","text":"**Exploring reqwest 0.13 features**\n\nI'm delving into the features of reqwest 0.13, especially its likely support for blocking. I wonder if I need the \"rustls\" feature specified as `rustls-tls`. I'll have to verify that later, but for now, I should gather documentation for `ReqwestDeltaDownloader`, especially regarding timeouts.\n\nThe `download_delta` method could use a timeout of 10 seconds and stream chunks, with progress callbacks that only trigger when percentages change by at least 1%. I’m thinking of using `std::time::Duration` and importing `reqwest::blocking::Client` for blocking operations. For files, I'm considering if the `download_delta` method could return a Vec."},{"type":"summary_text","text":"**Analyzing download failure**\n\nI'm noting a failed download situation with logs indicating a downloader failure. I need to look into why it didn't apply correctly. However, it seems the source backup is preserved, which is a silver lining. It’s possible that the patch isn't written, so I’ll need to explore further on that aspect. This will help me understand the problem better and find a solution."},{"type":"summary_text","text":"**Considering Cargo updates**\n\nI'm thinking it might be easier to edit Cargo before running `cargo test`, since that updates the lock file. The Cargo.lock should update automatically during the build unless it's frozen. I realize that the expected output needs to include Cargo.lock as part of the process. To manage dependencies effectively, I can use `cargo add` to include new ones. It's all about making sure everything is properly configured!"}]}

content.text: I’m going to add the xdelta3 binding dependency and enable reqwest’s blocking client so the production downloader/applier can be compiled and fixture-tested, then implement the traits and executor.

[package]
build = "build.rs"

content.text: The xdelta3 binding itself conflicts with Slint’s existing bindgen/clang dependency graph, so I won’t ship it. I’ll pivot to the pure-Rust VCDIFF decoder and prove it against a deterministic xdelta3-compatible fixture instead.

from pathlib import Path
import os
home=Path(os.environ.get('CARGO_HOME', Path.home()/'.cargo'))
root=list((home/'registry'/'src').glob('*/vcdiff-decoder-1.0.0'))[0]
print(root)
# print test constants/helpers names to find embedded fixtures
text=(root/'src/lib.rs').read_text(errors='ignore')
for i,line in enumerate(text.splitlines(),1):
    if 'patch' in line.lower() or 'source' in line.lower() or 'target' in line.lower() or 'assert_eq' in line:
        if 700 <= i <= 1050:
            print(f'{i}: {line[:220]}')
PY

arguments.purpose: Extract vcdiff-decoder unit test fixtures/API examples
arguments.script:
python - <<'PY'
from pathlib import Path
import os
home=Path(os.environ.get('CARGO_HOME', Path.home()/'.cargo'))
root=list((home/'registry'/'src').glob('*/vcdiff-decoder-1.0.0'))[0]
text=(root/'src/lib.rs').read_text(errors='ignore').splitlines()
for i,line in enumerate(text,1):
    if '#[test]' in line or 'fn test_' in line:
        print(f'\n{i}: {line}')
        for j in range(i+1, min(len(text), i+45)):
            print(f'{j}: {text[j-1][:220]}')
PY

content.thinking:
**Considering testing setup**

I’m thinking that the test shouldn’t rely on the network. I might consider checking the header of the patch manually, if that's accessible, but without including anything huge. Perhaps I could create a "tiny deterministic fixture" with a custom VCDIFF. For that, I need to use the vcdiff-decoder. So, it might be a good move to add it with cargo and remove xdelta3 from Cargo.toml. I’ll need to make those edits!

content.thinking:
**Verifying output CRC**

I think I need to verify the output CRC before considering it successful. It seems important because if there are any "malformed/failed apply" issues, the vcdiff might produce incorrect bytes. So, I'll definitely want to check that the CRC aligns with the target I'm aiming for in this plan. It feels like a crucial step to ensure everything works correctly!

**Considering file writing safety**

I'm thinking through the process of writing and verifying files. Before writing, I should compute the CRC to ensure it matches the desired value. If it doesn’t, I won’t write the file to avoid incorrect data. After successful output, I should handle cleanup, ensuring that old backups are removed if needed. I need to keep the paths safe while considering how future UIs might want to manage file paths. It’s all about maintaining a strict system for reliability!

**Developing patching logic**

I need to create a helper function for ensuring file CRCs and reading existing bytes. Additionally, it’s important to map platform errors to strings with a `safe_string` for easier handling. If there's a downloader failure, I shouldn't write anything. I also need to account for situations where a patch file exists but is unreadable—if that happens, I should fail gracefully.

content.text: I spotted an oversized generated no-op block in the restore helper; I’m replacing it with the intended concise restore logic before compiling.

arguments.purpose: Replace oversized restore helper with concise implementation

content.partialJson:
{"purpose":"Replace oversized restore helper with concise implementation","runtime":"python3","script":"from pathlib import Path
path = Path('src/services/downgrader.rs')
text = path.read_text()
start = text.index('    fn restore_desired_backup')
end = text.index('    fn download_apply_and_cleanup', start)
new = '''    fn restore_desired_backup(\n        &self,\n        row: &DowngraderPreviewPlanRow,\n        current_backup_path: &Path,\n        desired_backup_path: &Path,\n    ) -> Result<Vec<String>, String> {\n        let desired_crc = self.read_required_crc(desired_backup_path)?;\n        if !crc_is_desired_target_for_plan(&desired_crc, row.plan.target) {\n            return Err(format!(\n                "{} desired backup changed before restore.",\n                row.plan.display_name\n            ));\n        }\n        self.read_required_crc(current_backup_path)?;\n\n        let mut diagnostics = Vec::new();\n        if row.options().keep_backups {\n            self.filesystem\n                .copy_file(desired_backup_path, &row.current_path)\n                .map_err(|error| {\n                    mutation_diagnostic(\n                        row,\n                        DowngraderPlanStepKind::RestoreDesiredBackup,\n                        error.user_message(),\n                    )\n                })?;\n        } else {\n            self.filesystem\n                .rename_file(desired_backup_path, &row.current_path)\n                .map_err(|error| {\n                    mutation_diagnostic(\n                        row,\n                        DowngraderPlanStepKind::RestoreDesiredBackup,\n                        error.user_message(),\n                    )\n                })?;\n            self.remove_file_with_context(\n                current_backup_path,\n                row,\n                DowngraderPlanStepKind::DeleteCurrentBackup,\n            )?;\n        }\n        diagnostics.push(format!(\n            "{} restored from {}.",\n            row.plan.display_name, row.plan.desired_backup_name\n        ));\n        Ok(diagnostics)\n    }\n\n'''
path.write_text(text[:start] + new + text[end:])
print('restore helper replaced')","timeout_ms":10000}

content.thinking:
**Analyzing options for backup restoration**

I’m considering the options for handling backups, thinking perhaps it’s better to be direct rather than inferring too much. The private extension could seem odd, and I don’t need public documentation. I want to clarify the logic for keeping backups, differentiating between keeping the current and invalid backups, as well as the implications of restoration downloads. I'll need to address some potential compilation issues related to `reqwest` and types. Also, I need to ensure error handling during execution is robust.

**Preparing for backup operations**

I need to handle `download_apply...`, which reads from the source bytes after preparing the current backup. If the current backup is valid but the current file is missing, I should check whether a source backup exists. If the desired backup is deemed invalid after deletion and the download fails, I still have the backup intact. I'll need to perform a CRC check on the output, which could fail unless we have a known CRC for tests. I want to make sure tests are added and consider updating the `Cargo.lock` file before I run any checks.

content.text: With executor code in place, I’m appending focused executor tests using an in-memory writable filesystem, fake downloader, fake applier, and a deterministic VCDIFF fixture.

content.name: gsd_exec

arguments.purpose: Append downgrader executor fake seams and coverage tests

content.thinking:
**Considering unsupported sources**

I’m thinking about how to test for negative "unsupported source generation." The current file's byte status is unknown, and the target is oldgen. I plan to use row.steps like SkipUnsupportedVersion and maybe execute the skip. However, if the preview_plan has an unknown status with an action to validate, and the current CRC is unsupported, then the steps might just be to skip those. I wonder if I can actually execute that.

arguments.purpose: Refactor downgrader download/apply helper to avoid clippy too_many_arguments

content.partialJson:
{"purpose":"Refactor downgrader download/apply helper to avoid clippy too_many_arguments","runtime":"python","script":"from pathlib import Path
path=Path('src/services/downgrader.rs')
text=path.read_text()
# Replace the two call blocks with compact context construction. There are two identical-ish blocks.
old1='''                self.download_apply_and_cleanup(\n                    request_id,\n                    game_root,\n                    row,\n                    &current_backup_path,\n                    downloader,\n                    applier,\n                    progress_events,\n                    diagnostics,\n                )'''
new1='''                self.download_apply_and_cleanup(\n                    PatchApplyContext {\n                        request_id,\n                        game_root,\n                        row,\n                        current_backup_path: &current_backup_path,\n                        progress_events,\n                        diagnostics,\n                    },\n                    downloader,\n                    applier,\n                )'''
text=text.replace(old1,new1)
old2='''            CrcProbe::Missing => self.download_apply_and_cleanup(\n                request_id,\n                game_root,\n                row,\n                &current_backup_path,\n                downloader,\n                applier,\n                progress_events,\n                diagnostics,\n            ),'''
new2='''            CrcProbe::Missing => self.download_apply_and_cleanup(\n                PatchApplyContext {\n                    request_id,\n                    game_root,\n                    row,\n                    current_backup_path: &current_backup_path,\n                    progress_events,\n                    diagnostics,\n                },\n                downloader,\n                applier,\n            ),'''
text=text.replace(old2,new2)
start=text.index('    fn download_apply_and_cleanup')
end=text.index('    fn read_or_download_patch', start)
old_func=text[start:end]
new_func='''    fn download_apply_and_cleanup<D, A>(\n        &self,\n        mut context: PatchApplyContext<'_>,\n        downloader: &D,\n        applier: &A,\n    ) -> RowExecutionStatus\n    where\n        D: DeltaDownloader + ?Sized,\n        A: DeltaApplier + ?Sized,\n    {\n        let row = context.row;\n        let source_bytes = match self.filesystem.read_bytes(context.current_backup_path) {\n            Ok(bytes) => bytes,\n            Err(error) => {\n                return RowExecutionStatus::Failed {\n                    diagnostics: vec![mutation_diagnostic(\n                        row,\n                        DowngraderPlanStepKind::ApplyDeltaPatch,\n                        error.user_message(),\n                    )],\n                };\n            }\n        };\n        let patch_path = context.game_root.join(&row.plan.patch_name);\n        let patch_bytes = match self.read_or_download_patch(\n            context.request_id,\n            row,\n            &patch_path,\n            downloader,\n            context.progress_events,\n        ) {\n            Ok(bytes) => bytes,\n            Err(diagnostic) => {\n                return RowExecutionStatus::Failed {\n                    diagnostics: vec![diagnostic],\n                };\n            }\n        };\n        let output_bytes = match applier.apply_delta(&source_bytes, &patch_bytes) {\n            Ok(bytes) => bytes,\n            Err(error) => {\n                warn!(\n                    event = "downgrader-execute-apply-failed",\n                    request_id = context.request_id,\n                    relative_path = row.plan.relative_path,\n                    diagnostic = error.diagnostic(),\n                    "Downgrader delta apply failed"\n                );\n                return RowExecutionStatus::Failed {\n                    diagnostics: vec![format!(\n                        "{} {}",\n                        error.user_message(),\n                        error.diagnostic()\n                    )],\n                };\n            }\n        };\n        let output_crc = crc32_hex(&output_bytes);\n        if !crc_is_desired_target_for_plan(&output_crc, row.plan.target) {\n            return RowExecutionStatus::Failed {\n                diagnostics: vec![format!(\n                    "{} patch output CRC {output_crc} did not match target {}.",\n                    row.plan.display_name,\n                    row.plan.target.as_reference_str()\n                )],\n            };\n        }\n\n        if let Err(error) = self.filesystem.write_bytes(&row.current_path, &output_bytes) {\n            return RowExecutionStatus::Failed {\n                diagnostics: vec![mutation_diagnostic(\n                    row,\n                    DowngraderPlanStepKind::ApplyDeltaPatch,\n                    error.user_message(),\n                )],\n            };\n        }\n        if !row.options().keep_backups {\n            if let Err(diagnostic) = self.remove_file_with_context(\n                context.current_backup_path,\n                row,\n                DowngraderPlanStepKind::DeleteCurrentBackup,\n            ) {\n                return RowExecutionStatus::Failed {\n                    diagnostics: vec![diagnostic],\n                };\n            }\n        }\n        if row.options().delete_deltas {\n            if let Err(diagnostic) =\n                self.remove_file_with_context(&patch_path, row, DowngraderPlanStepKind::DeleteDeltaPatch)\n            {\n                return RowExecutionStatus::Failed {\n                    diagnostics: vec![diagnostic],\n                };\n            }\n        }\n\n        context.diagnostics.push(format!(\n            "{} patched with {}.",\n            row.plan.display_name, row.plan.patch_name\n        ));\n        RowExecutionStatus::Patched {\n            diagnostics: context.diagnostics,\n        }\n    }\n\n'''
text=text[:start]+new_func+text[end:]
insert=text.index('#[derive(Debug, Clone, PartialEq, Eq)]\nenum RowExecutionStatus')
ctx='''struct PatchApplyContext<'a> {\n    request_id: u64,\n    game_root: &'a Path,\n    row: &'a DowngraderPreviewPlanRow,\n    current_backup_path: &'a Path,\n    progress_events: &'a mut Vec<DowngraderExecutionProgressEvent>,\n    diagnostics: Vec<String>,\n}\n\n'''
if 'struct PatchApplyContext' not in text:
    text=text[:insert]+ctx+text[insert:]
path.write_text(text)
print('refactored download/apply helper')","timeout_ms":10000}
